name: Banner
description_short: Banner Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution serving Banner Health employees and families with competitive loan rates, savings accounts, and digital banking services.
description_long:
Banner Federal Credit Union operates as a specialized credit union exclusively serving Banner Health employees and their families. Founded to meet the banking needs of a major healthcare employer, the credit union has built a membership base with reported 94% member satisfaction rates. The institution maintains a physical presence in Phoenix, Arizona, with a headquarters location at 2909 N Central Ave, and offers virtual branch access for remote members.

The credit union provides a comprehensive suite of retail banking services including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), individual retirement accounts (IRAs), and a variety of loan products. Loan offerings include auto loans (5.49% APR), home equity loans (7.00%-7.50% APR), personal signature loans (10.99% APR), RV/boat loans (6.50% APR), and motorcycle loans (6.75% APR). Deposit products feature competitive rates, with 6-12 month CDs at 3.75% APY (minimum $10,000) and IRA accounts at 3.50% APY. The credit union recently added Zelle money transfer functionality and offers Round Up Savings programs.

Banner FCU distinguishes itself through deep integration with Banner Health's employee base, leveraging member relationships built on workplace familiarity and institutional trust. Their member satisfaction rate of over 94% is prominently featured, and customer testimonials emphasize responsive loan departments and friendly teller service. The organization maintains transparency through regular annual member meetings and actively recruits volunteer supervisory committee members, reflecting traditional credit union governance practices.

The primary limitation is that Banner FCU operates as a closed-field credit union, restricting membership to Banner Health employees and their families. This means the vast majority of consumers cannot join. While the website displays current rates and services, specific details about minimum account balances, account maintenance fees, and full product terms are limited. The credit union appears to be a legitimate, well-established institution but serves a narrow member base.
services: Checking accounts with digital banking access
Savings accounts and high-yield savings options
Certificates of Deposit (6-12 month terms, 3.75% APY)
Home equity loans (fixed and variable rate options)
Personal signature loans (10.99% APR starting rate)
RV and boat loans (6.50% APR starting rate)
pros: Competitive auto loan rates starting at 5.49% APR with options for motorcycles (6.75%) and RVs (6.50%)
Strong member satisfaction reported at over 94%, supported by specific customer testimonials
Competitive CD rates at 3.75% APY for 6-12 month terms (minimum $10,000)
Home equity loans available with both variable (7.00%) and fixed (7.50%) rate options
Digital banking access including virtual branch login and Zelle money transfer capability
Round Up Savings program for automated, incremental savings
Active member governance with annual meetings and supervisory committee volunteer opportunities
cons: Membership restricted exclusively to Banner Health employees and their families—not open to general public
Limited disclosure of account fees, minimum balance requirements, and complete product terms on website
Single primary location in Phoenix, Arizona, though virtual branch access is available
Website content does not detail customer support hours, response times, or technical support availability
Personal loan rates (10.99% APR) are higher than some alternatives, with limited pre-qualification information
best_for: Banner Health employees seeking employer-affiliated banking with competitive rates and member governance
Families of Banner Health workers looking to consolidate banking with a trusted credit union
Healthcare workers interested in employer-aligned financial services with strong community focus
Members prioritizing personal relationship banking and direct loan officer interaction
diagnosis: Banner Federal Credit Union is ideal for employees and families of Banner Health seeking employer-affiliated banking with competitive rates and strong service reviews. The critical caveat is that membership is restricted to Banner Health employees and their immediate families—making this credit union inaccessible to the general public. Prospective members must verify employer eligibility before pursuing membership.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Banner — Credit Union in Phoenix, AZ | CreditDoc
meta_description: Banner — NCUA-insured credit union in Phoenix, AZ. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
